comet_behavior: Task bootstrap advertises batched browser actions, ref-based targeting, and subagent creation to the backend.
current_repo_status: Current sidecar builds a local tool catalog and planner/executor phase trace, but exposes no create_subagent feature in the runtime contract.

comet_behavior: The browser-task runtime can launch subagents and marks them explicitly as subagent runs.
current_repo_status: No subagent creation tool or child task runtime exists in the current repo.

comet_behavior: The extension distinguishes mission-control tasks from primary tasks and subagents in lifecycle logic.
current_repo_status: No mission-control equivalent exists; current repo has one run type with pause/resume/stop.

comet_behavior: Spawnable subagents are proven, but the full backend graph size is not exposed by the captured client bundle.
evidence_excerpt: create_subagent is present, but no explicit planner graph or agent-count enumeration is exposed client-side.
current_repo_status: Current repo visibly runs one orchestrator loop with planner/executor phases.

comet_behavior: Comet uses dedicated browser-task messages for pause/resume, stop, completion, and progress screenshots.
current_repo_status: Current repo uses agent run RPC plus SSE updates and overlay messages, not a dedicated task message family.

comet_behavior: The extension tracks pending tasks, pending requests, and task-scoped loggers.
evidence_excerpt: pendingTasks = new Map(), pendingRequests = new Map(), taskScopedLoggers = new Map()
current_repo_status: Current repo tracks runs in memory inside one orchestrator but has no task-scoped socket/task registry.

comet_behavior: Comet reports activity-gap thresholds for live tasks while they remain pending.
current_repo_status: Current repo has loop guards and failure tolerance but no task inactivity watchdog at the control-plane level.

comet_behavior: Comet keeps a reconnecting WebSocket that only stays alive while tasks are pending.
evidence_excerpt: Socket closed with ... pending, attempting to reconnect ... closeConnectionIfAllTasksComplete()
current_repo_status: Current repo uses local HTTP/SSE and CDP sessions; it does not have a reconnectable task socket layer.

comet_behavior: Each pending task emits a reconnect message so the backend can reassociate task state after socket loss.
current_repo_status: Current repo reattaches CDP tab sessions locally, but does not reconnect agent tasks to a backend task socket.

comet_behavior: Comet can move an active task/thread into the sidecar with task-aware metadata.
current_repo_status: Current repo has panel and overlay control, but no task-thread move/handoff path tied to live task metadata.

comet_behavior: Comet supports low-level batched browser actuation through computer_batch.
current_repo_status: Current repo already supports batched browser actions through ComputerBatch/computer params.steps[] and similar timing semantics.

comet_behavior: Comet explicitly supports computer_ref for ref-driven action targeting.
current_repo_status: Current repo already supports ref-based routing in computer, form_input, and find.

comet_behavior: Subagent creation is a first-class tool entry point in the browser-task runtime.
current_repo_status: No equivalent tool exists in current transport or sidecar RPC surface.

comet_behavior: Comet provides full-page, visible-tab, and cropped partial screenshot capture paths through content/background coordination.
current_repo_status: Current repo supports screenshot capture inside browser actions and panel image attachment flows, but not the same standalone task tool family.

comet_behavior: Comet can activate cursor context and retrieve cursor-grounded page context directly from content script state.
current_repo_status: Current repo has overlay cursor visuals, but no equivalent standalone cursor-context tool exposed to the agent runtime.

capability: selection capture and Google Docs specialization
comet_behavior: Comet captures user selections and includes Google Docs-specific selection extraction logic.
current_repo_status: Current repo has screenshot and overlay tools, but no equivalent user-selection capture pipeline tied into task context.

comet_behavior: Comet offscreen helpers parse PDFs and exported Google Docs/Sheets content into agent-usable text.
current_repo_status: Current repo has page-reading tools but no matching offscreen document-helper surface in the browser-agent runtime.

comet_behavior: Subagent and mission-control tasks mark tabs hidden and clean them up on destroy.
evidence_excerpt: is_subagent || is_mission_control => isHidden = !0 ... isHidden && is_subagent && chrome.tabs.remove(t)
current_repo_status: Current repo does not create hidden child tabs for agent subtasks.

capability: initial tab ownership and auto-close sender behavior
comet_behavior: Comet can bootstrap from sender tab/start_url and may close the initial sender tab when appropriate, except for subagent and mission-control contexts.
current_repo_status: Current repo attaches to live tabs and opens the panel, but lacks a task-owned tabs manager with sender-tab ownership rules.

capability: task bootstrap page state and screenshot
comet_behavior: Task start payload includes tabs context and an initial current-tab screenshot.
current_repo_status: Current repo can capture screenshots and tab state, but does not package them into a task bootstrap contract equivalent to start_agent.

capability: JavaScript dialog handling at runtime boundary
comet_behavior: The tabs manager listens for Page.javascriptDialogOpening and auto-accepts dialogs immediately.
current_repo_status: Current repo tracks dialogs via target-event-router/session-registry and can handle them through browser-actions, but does not auto-accept by default.

comet_behavior: Browser agent mode is separately enabled, org-gated, and model-gated from other search modes.
current_repo_status: Current repo selects models at the extension/panel level but has no distinct browser-agent mode gate or allow-once policy layer.

comet_behavior: Comet supports a dedicated allow-once confirmation action for browser-agent permissioning.
current_repo_status: Current repo has overlay stop/pause controls but no dedicated browser-agent allow-once permission flow.

comet_behavior: Sidecar web resources track pending tasks and navigation metadata and use them in debug/task UI flows.
current_repo_status: Current repo panel is aware of one current run and overlay state, not a multi-task sidecar store.

comet_behavior: Comet visibly operates with richer task/subagent orchestration beyond a simple phase label.
evidence_excerpt: PlannerAgent / ExecutorAgent exist locally, while Comet exposes create_subagent and is_subagent task behavior.
current_repo_status: Current repo has planner/executor interfaces but no independent planner runtime, no subagent runtime, and no handoff contract between separate task actors.

comet_behavior: Comet’s client implies server-backed task planning, decomposition, and task shell execution rather than local heuristic prompt parsing.
evidence_excerpt: Current planner parses prompt heuristically; Comet client mainly boots tasks and tools rather than exposing local planning logic.
current_repo_status: Current repo planner is a heuristic extractor that converts prompt text into deterministic steps before the main provider loop.

capability: single provider transcript for planning, acting, and answering
comet_behavior: Comet’s captured browser-task runtime separates task/socket orchestration from UI state and subagent spawning.
evidence_excerpt: Current repo mixes planning, tool use, loop guards, validation, and final answer in one transcript; Comet manages tasks outside the UI transcript.
current_repo_status: This is the main current-repo architectural bottleneck for browser-task competence.

comet_behavior: Comet exposes pause/resume from overlay controls and task status changes.
current_repo_status: Current repo already has a solid reassessment-on-resume behavior and should keep it.

comet_behavior: Comet overlay offers pause/resume/stop and animated status on the page surface.
evidence_excerpt: Resume Comet Assistant / Pause Comet Assistant / Stop
current_repo_status: Current repo already has strong overlay controls and status forwarding through panel.js, agent-overlay.js, and background.js.
